div1.entryFree: Fork, any thing dividing into two: thou dost fear the soft and tender f. of a poor worm, Meas. III, 1, 16 (i. e. the forked tongue of a snake). adder's f. Mcb. IV, 1, 16. though the f. invade the region of my heart, Lr. I, 1, 146 (i. e. the barbed arrow-head). whose face between her --s presages snow, IV, 6, 121 (i. e. between her legs; cf. III, 4, 113).
